zh.Stapleford, Cambridgeshire, England: 英國英格蘭坎布利其爾斯塔普利福德
zh.lifeSummary: Agnes Tiler於大約1555年出生在英國艾可貝爾豪斯。她於1578年與John Ansell結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1650年之前。

zh-hans.Stapleford, Cambridgeshire, England: 英国英格兰坎布利其爾斯塔普利福德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Agnes Tiler于大约1555年出生在英国艾可貝爾豪斯。她于1578年与John Ansell结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1650年之前。

nameMeanings.name: Tiler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Tellwright
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English tile ‘tile’ + wright(e) ‘wright’ (Old English tigelwyrhta), denoting someone who made tiles. Compare Tiler , Tillman .

variants.name: Tyler
meanings: English: occupational name from Old French tieuleor, tiewelier, tuilier, Middle English tiler(e), tiʒeler ‘tiler’, for someone who made or laid tiles for floors or roofs. This name may have become confused with Tiller . Wat Tyler was a leader of the English Peasants' Revolt in 1381, although his name may not have been hereditary at the time.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Agnes Tiler was born about 1555, in Bellhouse, Essex, England. She married John Ansell in 1578. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died before 1650.

fr.lifeSummary: Agnes Tiler est née environ 1555, à Bellhouse, Essex, Angleterre. Elle a épousé John Ansell en 1578. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée avant 1650.

de.lifeSummary: Agnes Tiler wurde um 1555 in Bellhouse, Essex, England geboren. Sie hat 1578 John Ansell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor 1650.

it.lifeSummary: Agnes Tiler è nata il circa 1555 nel Bellhouse, Essex, England. Lei sposato John Ansell nel 1578. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì prima del 1650.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Tilerは約1555年にイギリスエセックスベルハウゼで生まれた。1578年にJohn Ansellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1650年以前に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Tiler(은)는 대략 1555년 영국 에세스 브하스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1578년 John Ansell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1650년 이전 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Agnes Tiler nasceu aproximadamente 1555, em Bellhouse, Essex, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com John Ansell em 1578. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 1650.

ru.lifeSummary: Agnes Tiler родилась около 1555 г. в местности беллхоус, эссекс, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Ansell в 1578 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла перед 1650 г.
